one_time_description: Loss on impairment of capital assets recorded in 2021; also prior period adjustment for professional fees incorrectly expensed instead of capitalized related to investment in GBEs
economic_notes: Glooscap Corner operates convenience store, gaming facility and gas bar. Commercial fisheries operates three lobster fishing boats. GBEs include Landing #2 (convenience store), Glooscap Energy (renewable energy), Yarmouth Bar Fisheries (seafood processor, 52% ownership), Seakist Lobster (lobster pound). Feta cheese operation ceased during year. Post year-end: purchased 5 Lobster Fishing Area 34 licenses for $3,500,000 with $1,000,000 ISC grant; ATR process for three land parcels received conditional ministerial approval June 2021. COVID-19 impacted Corner, fisheries and Landing #2 operations.
notes: Prior period adjustment restated 2020 figures for professional fees incorrectly expensed instead of capitalized (increased investment in GBEs by $73,267). Economic Development and Landing #1 changed status from GBEs to fully consolidated entities during the year, resulting in transfer of $4,867,681 in tangible capital assets into consolidated statements. Grant payable of $768,602 to ISC related to Glooscap Ilia Gourmet Foods feta cheese equipment grant. Pension plan errors identified with $146,688 liability recorded. Change in accounting policy for intangible assets (swordfish license $340,000 retrospectively capitalized). COVID-19 material uncertainty note included. Segment disclosure does not exactly reconcile housing expenses into single line; $245,654 estimated from Band Housing and CMHC Housing segments combined ($28,253 + $217,401). Expenses by object note appears truncated in source document. No schedule of remuneration provided.
